title: What Does My Daily Money Rhythm Look Like?
subtitle: Journal prompts for finding a money habit that fits your life
excerpt: Five journal prompts for examining your relationship with daily money habits. The warm-up questions start wide before the closer prompts look at what's actually in place and what the next step might be.
sourceNotes: Adapted from Day 50 of The Money Journal: 12 Weeks to Peace, Freedom & Overflow (Rebecca J Page, 2025, ISBN 9798276631448). The prompts draw on the MONEY Journal Week 8 daily reflection questions.

practice.whenToUse: Use when you want to understand what's stopped your money habits from sticking, or when you're ready to build one.

content.text: Five journal prompts looking at your daily money rhythm: what it currently is, where it breaks down, and what a sustainable version could look like. Set a timer for twenty minutes. Write without editing. Stop when the timer goes.
New to journalling as practice? Read
What is my relationship with daily money habits right now?
Write one or two sentences on where things stand. No judgement required, just a current-state picture.
What routines already support my financial wellbeing, even in small ways?
Look for what's already there before asking what's missing.
What daily or weekly habits could make wealth feel consistent and reliable for me?
Specific habits, not aspirations. 'Check the balance at 8am' rather than 'be more organised with money.'
What stops me from practising these habits?
Write the real reason, not the polished one.
What does my daily money rhythm look like when it's working?
Picture a specific morning or day. What are you doing? When? For how long?
Adapted from Day 50 of The Money Journal: 12 Weeks to Peace, Freedom & Overflow (Rebecca J Page, 2025), Week 8 daily reflection prompts.
